name: Genesis
author: Traditionally Moses
date: approx. 1445-1405 BC (traditional); some scholarly opinion places final composition/editing in the exilic or postexilic period
period: Creation through the patriarchs
timelinePlacement: Creation to Joseph's death

connections.note: Genesis connects with Exodus through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Genesis connects with Romans through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Exodus
author: Traditionally Moses
date: approx. 1445-1405 BC (traditional); some scholarly opinion places final form later in Israel's history
period: Israel's deliverance from Egypt and covenant at Sinai
timelinePlacement: From slavery in Egypt to the tabernacle at Sinai

connections.note: Exodus connects with Genesis through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Exodus connects with Leviticus through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Leviticus
author: Traditionally Moses
date: approx. 1445-1405 BC (traditional); some scholarly opinion places final priestly shaping later
period: Sinai covenant worship and holiness
timelinePlacement: At Mount Sinai after the tabernacle is established

connections.note: Leviticus connects with Exodus through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Leviticus connects with Hebrews through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Numbers
author: Traditionally Moses
date: approx. 1445-1405 BC (traditional); some scholarly opinion places final form later
period: Israel's wilderness generation
timelinePlacement: approx. 1445-1405 BC from Sinai to Moab

connections.note: Numbers connects with Leviticus through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Numbers connects with Deuteronomy through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Deuteronomy
author: Traditionally Moses; final notice of Moses' death may reflect later inspired editorial work
date: approx. 1405 BC (traditional); some scholarly opinion places significant composition or editing in the monarchic/exilic period
period: Moses' covenant renewal on the plains of Moab
timelinePlacement: Moses' final speeches before Israel enters Canaan
outline: 5-11 Covenant commands and love for God
12-26 Laws for life in the land

connections.note: Deuteronomy connects with Numbers through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Deuteronomy connects with Joshua through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Joshua
author: Anonymous; traditionally linked with Joshua and later prophetic/editorial sources
date: events approx. 1405-1375 BC; final composition uncertain
period: Conquest and settlement of Canaan
timelinePlacement: approx. 1405-1375 BC

connections.note: Joshua connects with Deuteronomy through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Joshua connects with Judges through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Judges
author: Anonymous; traditionally associated with Samuel
date: events approx. 1375-1050 BC; composition likely during or after the early monarchy
period: Cycles of apostasy, oppression, deliverance, and relapse
timelinePlacement: approx. 1375-1050 BC

connections.note: Judges connects with Joshua through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Judges connects with Ruth through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Ruth
author: Anonymous; traditionally associated with Samuel
date: events during the judges, approx. 12th-11th century BC; composition likely during the monarchy
period: Family redemption in the days of the judges
timelinePlacement: During the judges, leading toward David's line
outline: 4 Boaz redeems and David's line is traced

connections.note: Ruth connects with Judges through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Ruth connects with 1 Samuel through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: 1 Samuel
author: Anonymous; traditionally associated with Samuel, Nathan, and Gad as sources
date: events approx. 1100-1010 BC; final composition after the kingdom divided
period: Transition from judges to monarchy
timelinePlacement: Samuel's birth through Saul's decline and David's rise

connections.note: 1 Samuel connects with Ruth through shared history, theology, or literary development.
1 Samuel connects with 2 Samuel through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: 2 Samuel
author: Anonymous; traditionally associated with Samuel, Nathan, and Gad as sources
date: events approx. 1010-970 BC; final composition after David's reign
period: United monarchy under David
timelinePlacement: David's reign over Judah and Israel

connections.note: 2 Samuel connects with 1 Samuel through shared history, theology, or literary development.
2 Samuel connects with 1 Kings through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: 1 Kings
author: Anonymous; Jewish tradition associates Kings with Jeremiah
date: events approx. 970-853 BC; final form during or after the exile
period: Solomon and the divided kingdom
timelinePlacement: Solomon's reign through early divided monarchy

connections.note: 1 Kings connects with 2 Samuel through shared history, theology, or literary development.
1 Kings connects with 2 Kings through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: 2 Kings
author: Anonymous; Jewish tradition associates Kings with Jeremiah
date: events approx. 853-561 BC; final form during or after the exile
period: Divided kingdom, exile of Israel, and fall of Judah
timelinePlacement: Elijah's departure to Jerusalem's fall and exile

connections.note: 2 Kings connects with 1 Kings through shared history, theology, or literary development.
2 Kings connects with Jeremiah through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: 1 Chronicles
author: Anonymous; traditionally associated with Ezra or the Chronicler
period: Genealogies and David's reign retold for restored Judah
timelinePlacement: Adam to David's preparations for the temple
outline: 1-9 Genealogies from Adam to restored Judah

connections.note: 1 Chronicles connects with 2 Samuel through shared history, theology, or literary development.
1 Chronicles connects with 2 Chronicles through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: 2 Chronicles
author: Anonymous; traditionally associated with Ezra or the Chronicler
period: Solomon, Judah's kings, exile, and return hope
timelinePlacement: Solomon's reign to Cyrus' decree
outline: 10-28 Judah's kings from Rehoboam to Ahaz
33-36 Judah's final kings, exile, and Cyrus' decree

connections.note: 2 Chronicles connects with 1 Chronicles through shared history, theology, or literary development.
2 Chronicles connects with Ezra through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Ezra
author: Anonymous; traditionally associated with Ezra
period: Return from exile and temple restoration

connections.note: Ezra connects with 2 Chronicles through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Ezra connects with Nehemiah through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Ezra connects with Haggai through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Nehemiah
author: Anonymous; traditionally associated with Nehemiah and Ezra
period: Jerusalem's wall rebuilding and covenant renewal

connections.note: Nehemiah connects with Ezra through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Nehemiah connects with Malachi through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Esther
author: Anonymous
date: events approx. 483-473 BC; composition likely Persian or early Hellenistic period
period: Jewish preservation in Persia
timelinePlacement: During the Persian Empire before or around Ezra's return

connections.note: Esther connects with Ezra through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Esther connects with Daniel through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Job
author: Anonymous
date: unknown; often placed in patriarchal setting, with composition proposed from early to postexilic periods
period: Wisdom reflection on suffering and God's sovereignty
timelinePlacement: Narrative setting outside Israel's national timeline, often viewed as patriarchal-era

connections.note: Job connects with Psalms through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Job connects with James through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Psalms
author: Multiple authors; many traditionally attributed to David, with Asaph, sons of Korah, Solomon, Moses, and anonymous psalms
date: approx. 15th-5th centuries BC, collected over time
period: Israel's worship, lament, wisdom, and royal hope
timelinePlacement: From Moses through postexilic worship collection

connections.note: Psalms connects with 2 Samuel through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Psalms connects with Hebrews through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Proverbs
author: Primarily Solomon; also sayings of Agur, Lemuel, and other wise contributors
date: core Solomonic material approx. 10th century BC; final collection likely later monarchic period
period: Wisdom instruction for covenant life
timelinePlacement: Wisdom collection associated chiefly with Solomon's era and later editors
outline: 25-29 More Solomonic proverbs collected under Hezekiah

connections.note: Proverbs connects with 1 Kings through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Proverbs connects with Ecclesiastes through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Ecclesiastes
author: Anonymous voice of Qoheleth; traditionally associated with Solomon
date: traditional Solomonic setting approx. 10th century BC; many scholars date composition later, possibly postexilic
period: Wisdom meditation on life, mortality, and fearing God
timelinePlacement: Wisdom setting associated with Solomon, with debated final composition
outline: 1-2 Vanity and the search for meaning

connections.note: Ecclesiastes connects with Proverbs through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Ecclesiastes connects with Song of Solomon through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Song of Solomon
author: Traditionally Solomon
date: traditional Solomonic date approx. 10th century BC; some scholarly opinion places final form later
period: Poetic celebration of covenant love
timelinePlacement: Wisdom/poetic setting associated with Solomon's era

connections.note: Song of Solomon connects with 1 Kings through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Song of Solomon connects with Ecclesiastes through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Isaiah
author: Isaiah son of Amoz; scholarly opinion often discusses later editorial shaping, especially for chapters 40-66
date: ministry approx. 740-680 BC (traditional); many scholars place parts of final composition in exilic/postexilic periods
period: Judah under Assyrian threat with promises of comfort and restoration
timelinePlacement: 8th-century BC ministry with future restoration vision
outline: 13-27 Oracles against nations and future hope

connections.note: Isaiah connects with 2 Kings through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Isaiah connects with Matthew through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Jeremiah
author: Jeremiah, with Baruch associated as scribe
period: Judah's final decades and the fall of Jerusalem
timelinePlacement: Late 7th to early 6th century BC
outline: 30-33 Book of comfort and new covenant

connections.note: Jeremiah connects with 2 Kings through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Jeremiah connects with Lamentations through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Lamentations
author: Anonymous; traditionally attributed to Jeremiah
date: shortly after Jerusalem's fall, approx. 586 BC
period: Mourning Jerusalem's destruction
timelinePlacement: After the fall of Jerusalem in 586 BC

connections.note: Lamentations connects with Jeremiah through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Lamentations connects with 2 Kings through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Ezekiel
author: Ezekiel the priest-prophet
period: Exile in Babylon with judgment and restoration visions
timelinePlacement: Early 6th century BC among the exiles

Ezekiel connects with Revelation through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Daniel
author: Traditionally Daniel; scholarly opinion often dates final form to the 2nd century BC
date: traditional setting and authorship approx. 6th century BC; critical dating often approx. 167-164 BC for final form
period: Exile and visions of God's kingdom over empires
timelinePlacement: Babylonian and Persian courts, with visions reaching beyond them

Daniel connects with Revelation through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Hosea
author: Hosea son of Beeri
period: Northern kingdom's final generation
timelinePlacement: Before the fall of Samaria in 722 BC

Hosea connects with Amos through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Joel
author: Joel son of Pethuel
date: uncertain; proposals range from early monarchic to postexilic, often approx. 9th-5th century BC
period: Day of the Lord, repentance, and Spirit promise
timelinePlacement: Undated prophetic crisis in Judah

connections.note: Joel connects with Acts through shared history, theology, or literary development.
Joel connects with Zephaniah through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Amos
author: Amos of Tekoa

Amos connects with Acts through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Obadiah
author: Obadiah
date: uncertain; often linked either to 9th-century conflict with Edom or to the aftermath of 586 BC
period: Judgment on Edom and hope for Zion
timelinePlacement: A crisis involving Edom and Jerusalem, date debated

Obadiah connects with Malachi through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Jonah
author: Anonymous; traditionally associated with Jonah son of Amittai
date: Jonah's ministry often placed in the 8th century BC; composition date debated
period: Prophetic mission to Nineveh
timelinePlacement: Assyrian period, before Nineveh's later fall

Jonah connects with Matthew through shared history, theology, or literary development.

name: Micah
author: Micah of Moresheth
period: Judah and Israel under threat from Assyria

Micah connects with Matthew through shared history, theology, or literary development.

author: Nahum the Elkoshite

author: Zephaniah son of Cushi

Zephaniah connects with Joel through shared history, theology, or literary development.

author: Haggai

Mark connects with 1 Peter through shared history, theology, or literary development.

author: Traditionally Luke the physician and companion of Paul

author: Traditionally John the apostle

name: Romans
author: Paul
period: Paul's mature exposition of the gospel to Rome

name: 1 Corinthians
author: Paul
period: Paul addresses church disorders and resurrection hope

author: Paul

period: Perseverance, correction, and the day of the Lord

name: 1 Timothy
author: Paul
date: approx. AD 62-65; some scholarly opinion questions Pauline authorship and dates later

name: Hebrews
author: Unknown; suggestions include Paul, Barnabas, Apollos, Luke, or Priscilla, but the book itself is anonymous
date: approx. AD 60-90, likely before or around the temple's destruction according to many proposals
period: Christ's superiority and perseverance in faith
timelinePlacement: Early Christian exhortation to believers with deep Old Testament grounding

Hebrews connects with Psalms through shared history, theology, or literary development.

author: Traditionally James, brother of Jesus

author: Peter

1 Peter connects with 2 Peter through shared history, theology, or literary development.

author: Traditionally John the apostle

author: Traditionally John the apostle, writing as the elder

2 John connects with 3 John through shared history, theology, or literary development.

author: Traditionally John the apostle, writing as the elder

author: John; traditionally John the apostle exiled on Patmos
